author: @alejandrodelvalle4083
text: Hello!! I really enjoy your videos and admire your work and enthusiasm! I would love to see the final results; and any tests on a real site. see how much power can we actually produce with such device. BTW its a neat little gadget! I am invited to go to Cameroon to make such things; I would lead the focus of some pedagogic experiences to play around with simple science; this is a beautiful example! There we will have to make it with soda bottles and recuperated copper; magnets will be trickier but we will manage! Thanks for sharying!!!

author: @TomKenny-bs3sk
text:
Hi,

I love your videos.

I run an activity centre featuring some quite long zip wires, that we send 100s of participants a day down. I have been toying with the idea of creating a device that can be fitted to a roller (device that runs down the cable) to power an led light system in the departure tower. I was wondering if it would be possible to get in touch and ask you a couple of questions?

author: @Nera-whimwhams
text: I think this is my favorite video you have made. You have such a great way of teaching, inspiring, and encouraging innovation...all while making me laugh. I truly appreciate you and the information you are sharing. Thank you❤

author: @lonewolfnmoon
text: Can multiple increasing magnetic coils/windings be used as braking mechanisms on wind turbines, etc.? So that at certain speeds a secondary, third, and so on activates; increasing windings - increases load. Using that as the braking mechanisms on wind turbines, etc., not only slowing them down but increasing their energy output?

author: @kennethcfogarty
text:
Star / Delta —— join the neg side together = star 
Connect in series = Delta 

Use a timer to start a high torque = Star
Timer :::::
Connect to Delta format and the full phase voltage across each coil = higher revolution

author: @mikaelfransson3658
text: In this film I can explain the odd magnet, I want to use half length of magnets and split them on both sides! NORTH in bottom and NORTH on the OTHER side. And same with south! Now we can use an extra magnet in between to get the generator to be fixed or levitated! /Mikael
